text: Continuous Integration, or CI, is a development practice where team members merge their code changes into a shared repository frequently. Instead of waiting days to integrate work, developers commit small updates multiple times a day. Each commit triggers an automated build—the system compiles the code automatically. CI pipelines then run a suite of tests to verify that the new code works correctly with the existing codebase. This approach helps by catching issues early, right after each change is made, rather than discovering problems later in the development process. By finding and fixing bugs quickly, teams can improve software quality, reduce integration problems, and deliver updates more efficiently.
